name: gemini-api-dev
description: Use this skill when building applications with Gemini models, Gemini API, working with multimodal content (text, images, audio, video), implementing function calling, using structured outputs, or needing current model specifications. Covers SDK usage (google-genai for Python, @google/genai for JavaScript/TypeScript), model selection, and API capabilities.
capabilities: Access latest Gemini 3.0 models including Pro and Flash variants
Implement multimodal content processing for text, images, audio, and video
Configure function calling to enable model-driven API invocations
Generate structured JSON outputs matching custom schemas
Utilize context caching for improved performance with large contexts
Generate text embeddings for semantic search applications
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 45 patterns that are all false positives. This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) containing code examples in markdown format. Backticks are markdown code fences, not shell execution. URLs are documentation references to official Google sources. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Developers need clear guidance on integrating Google's advanced AI models into their applications. This skill provides comprehensive documentation with SDK examples for Python, JavaScript, and Go to accelerate Gemini API development.
userTitle: Build apps with Google Gemini API
limitations: This skill provides documentation guidance only, not executable API code
Requires separate Google API key and billing setup through Google AI Studio
Does not cover deprecated models like gemini-2.x, gemini-1.5.x series
Code examples require manual implementation in your development environment

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers building AI-enhanced web applications
useCases.title: AI-powered application development
useCases.description: Build chatbots, content generators, and intelligent assistants using Gemini's advanced language capabilities with production-ready SDK patterns

useCases.targetUser: Data scientists and ML engineers working with diverse content types
useCases.title: Multimodal content analysis
useCases.description: Process and analyze images, documents, audio, and video content alongside text prompts for comprehensive understanding

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers creating intelligent automation systems
useCases.title: Automated workflow integration
useCases.description: Implement function calling to enable Gemini models to trigger external APIs, database queries, and business logic automatically

promptTemplates.title: Basic text generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Gemini API to generate a response. Initialize the client with your API key, call generate_content with model 'gemini-3-flash-preview', and pass your text prompt. Handle the response text output.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick start for simple chat or completion tasks

promptTemplates.title: Image analysis with Gemini
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up multimodal input by combining text and image data. Use the appropriate content parts for image data (base64 or URI), add your text question about the image, and send to a multimodal-capable model like gemini-3-pro-preview.
promptTemplates.scenario: Analyzing uploaded images, diagrams, or screenshots

promptTemplates.title: Function calling implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Define your function schema with name, description, and parameters. Pass function_declarations to the API config. When the model responds with function_call, execute your function and return results. Continue the conversation with the function response.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building AI agents that can query databases or call external APIs

promptTemplates.title: Structured JSON output
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure response_mime_type to 'application/json' and provide a response_schema defining your expected JSON structure. The model will generate valid JSON matching your schema with proper types and required fields.
promptTemplates.scenario: Extracting structured data from unstructured text or generating API-ready payloads

bestPractices: Always use v1beta API version as the default unless specifically required to use v1
Refer to the official REST API discovery spec as the source of truth for field names and types
Fetch the llms.txt documentation index to discover available API features and endpoints
Migrate from deprecated SDKs (google-generativeai, @google/generative-ai) to new packages
antiPatterns: Using legacy model names like gemini-2.0 or gemini-1.5 which are deprecated
Hardcoding API keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables
Assuming all Gemini models support the same features without checking model capabilities
Ignoring rate limits and quota constraints when making production API calls

faq.question: Which Gemini models should I use for new projects?
faq.answer: Use gemini-3-pro-preview for complex reasoning and coding tasks, or gemini-3-flash-preview for fast, cost-effective responses. Avoid deprecated 2.x and 1.5.x models.

faq.question: How do I get an API key for Gemini?
faq.answer: Visit Google AI Studio, create a project, and generate an API key. Free tier includes generous quotas for development and testing.

faq.question: What is the difference between v1 and v1beta API versions?
faq.answer: v1beta is the default and recommended version with latest features. Use v1 only when your integration specifically requires stable API behavior without beta features.

faq.question: Can Gemini process images and documents?
faq.answer: Yes, Gemini supports multimodal input including images, audio, video, PDFs, and text documents. Use the appropriate content parts to include non-text data in your requests.

faq.question: How does function calling work with Gemini?
faq.answer: Define your function schema in the API request. When Gemini determines a function should be called, it returns a function_call response. Execute your function and send results back to continue the conversation.

faq.question: What SDK should I use for my programming language?
faq.answer: Use google-genai for Python, @google/genai for JavaScript/TypeScript, or google.golang.org/genai for Go. Legacy packages google-generativeai and @google/generative-ai are deprecated.
